title: İşte Bu Parça!
subtitle: A robot yells at you. You sort trash. You can escape... Really!
team: Fabalu · dejw · Oskiek · Antik Karmaşa · Eleven
specs: 72 hours (I slept for maybe 6 of them)
Extintial Crisis Coordinator + Code + Art Direction
Grab gizmo → read its colour → yeet it into the correct machine
like you are being yelled at by a robot, which is exactly what it is

gallery.caption: The sorting rules, as a blueprint. Green = raw, orange = faulty, red = trash. Black = actually useful (rare).
Tools of the trade: a hook, a screwdriver, and a hammer for emotional support.
The reactor waits. The reactor is always hungry.

body: Picture this: a robot, a two-way radio, one (1) human, and an absolutely unreasonable pile of junk. The robot has opinions about where every piece of that junk should go. Your job is to agree with the robot. Quickly. This is İşte Bu Parça! — Turkish for roughly "That's the piece!", which is also the sound I made at 4 a.m. finding the bug that had eaten my evening.
I built the whole thing solo in 72 hours for Mağara Jam '23 — code, 3D models in Maya, design, the lot — and somehow it walked away with 2nd place. I'm still a little smug about it.

You're a first-person factory grunt. Gizmos drop onto your table, each glowing a colour that tells you its fate: green is raw material for the 3D printer, orange is a faulty part headed for the melter, red is straight-up trash for the shredder, and black means — miracle of miracles — it's actually ready to use. You've got a hook, a screwdriver and a hammer, a ticking clock, and a robot supervisor who never, ever stops talking.

Mostly that scope is a liar. Every jam I promise myself "small and polished this time," and every jam I end up hand-modelling a screwdriver at midnight because it had to feel right in the hand. Doing it alone meant every problem — physics jank, a reactor that refused to accept perfectly good trash, UI that fought me — was mine and only mine. No one to blame, no one to wait on. Turns out that's exactly the constraint I do my best work under.
If you play one thing I've made in a weekend, make it this one. Bring headphones. The robot insists.
